ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general mochi entry (FOODON_03301710). No specific entry exists for strawberry cream daifuku; closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP covers mochi/rice cake as a category. No specific daifuku or strawberry cream daifuku code exists; closest_parent applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for daifuku or strawberry cream mochi confectionery.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is 2 pieces of strawberry cream daifuku, weighing approximately 100 g in total (approximately 50 g per piece).

nutrient_source.title: Mochi, prepared from glutinous rice; cream-filled confection with strawberry — estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for mochi (NDB 20052) and whipped cream, combined with strawberry composition data, weighted by typical daifuku formulation proportions.
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for strawberry cream daifuku exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods, or FAO/INFOODS SEA. Values are estimated from a weighted composite of: (1) glutinous rice mochi skin (~55% by weight, USDA SR Legacy NDB 20052 mochi), (2) sweetened whipped cream filling (~30% by weight, USDA SR Legacy NDB 01053 cream, whipped), and (3) fresh strawberry (~15% by weight, USDA SR Legacy NDB 09316 strawberries, raw). Proportions are based on standard daifuku construction references in food science literature. Energy calculated by Atwater factors.

gi_source.title: Mochi (glutinous rice cake) — GI value from University of Sydney GI Database; analogous estimate applied to strawberry cream daifuku based on dominant glutinous rice mochi skin component.
gi_source.source_match_notes: The University of Sydney GI Database reports a GI of approximately 61 for mochi (glutinous rice cake). Strawberry cream daifuku is composed predominantly (~55%) of glutinous rice mochi skin, with cream and strawberry filling moderating the overall glycemic response. The GI of 61 (medium) is applied as an analogous estimate for the whole product. Glycemic load calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (61 × 36.0) / 100 ≈ 22.0 per 100 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (~28 mg per 100 g)
hypertension.guidance: Strawberry cream daifuku is low in sodium (~28 mg/100 g), making it unlikely to adversely affect blood pressure when consumed as an occasional treat within a balanced, sodium-controlled diet.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate and sugar content with medium-high glycemic index (GI ~61) and high glycemic load (~22 per serving)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit or avoid strawberry cream daifuku due to its high sugar content (~16.5 g/100 g), refined glutinous rice base (GI ~61), and high glycemic load (~22 per 100 g serving), which can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; portion control and infrequent consumption are strongly advised.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat from whipped cream filling (~4.3 g/100 g) and added sugar content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The whipped cream filling contributes moderate saturated fat (~4.3 g/100 g), and the high added sugar content may adversely affect lipid profiles and cardiovascular risk markers; individuals with cardiovascular disease should treat this as an occasional indulgence and limit serving frequency.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Strawberry Cream Daifuku (いちごクリーム大福, Ichigo Kuriimu Daifuku) is a Japanese confectionery (wagashi) consisting of a soft, chewy glutinous rice cake (mochi) skin encasing a filling of sweetened whipped cream and a whole or halved fresh strawberry. The mochi skin is made from glutinous rice flour (mochiko) steamed or microwaved with sugar and water, then hand-formed around the cream and fruit filling. The exterior is lightly dusted with cornstarch or potato starch to prevent sticking. The texture is distinctively soft and elastic from the mochi, contrasting with the light, airy cream and the juicy, slightly tart strawberry centre. The flavour profile is mildly sweet with a fresh fruity note. Strawberry daifuku (いちご大福) became popular in Japan in the 1980s and is now a widely enjoyed seasonal and year-round wagashi. In Singapore, it is served at Japanese dessert and sushi restaurants such as Sushi Express. Nutritionally, a 2-piece serving (~100 g) provides approximately 220 kcal, 36 g carbohydrate, 16.5 g sugar, 7 g fat (4.3 g saturated), and 2.5 g protein. The high refined carbohydrate and sugar content, combined with a medium glycemic index (~61) and high glycemic load (~22), makes this a high-concern food for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or insulin resistance. The moderate saturated fat from whipped cream warrants attention for cardiovascular health. Sodium is low (~28 mg/100 g), posing minimal hypertension risk. This food is vegetarian and halal-suitable but contains dairy.
common_user_queries: How many calories in strawberry cream daifuku?
How much sugar is in strawberry cream mochi?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for strawberry cream daifuku exists in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, FAO/INFOODS SEA). Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted composite of USDA SR Legacy entries for mochi, whipped cream, and fresh strawberry using standard daifuku formulation proportions. GI is an analogous estimate based on the University of Sydney GI Database mochi entry. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted composite of USDA SR Legacy data for mochi (NDB 20052), whipped cream (NDB 01053), and fresh strawberry (NDB 09316) at approximate proportions of 55:30:15 by weight. These are best-effort estimates pending an exact branded or laboratory analysis entry. 2. vitamin_d_mcg is set to -1 as it is not reliably reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entries used for this composite estimate. 3. selenium_mcg is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entries used for this composite estimate. 4. GI of 61 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database mochi entry; the actual GI of strawberry cream daifuku may differ depending on cream and sugar proportions. 5. Serving size of 100 g (2 pieces at ~50 g each) is an approximation based on typical commercial daifuku portion weights; actual product weight at Sushi Express Funan may vary. 6. Halal status is assumed based on typical ingredients; consumers should verify with the specific restaurant. 7. The food_id GD001946 is from the GrabFood Singapore seed record and is retained for traceability.

improvement_actions_taken: Constructed full RESULT JSON from seed GrabFood record with no prior nutrient data.
Estimated nutrient values from USDA SR Legacy composite (mochi NDB 20052, whipped cream NDB 01053, strawberry NDB 09316) at 55:30:15 weight proportions.
Applied GI of 61 as analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database mochi entry.
Added Japanese local name いちごクリーム大福 and romanized form Ichigo Kuriimu Daifuku to satisfy G6 Japanese cuisine multilingual requirement.
Documented -1 sentinel values for vitamin_d_mcg and selenium_mcg in notes per G3 sentinel documentation rule.
Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases consistency — all aliases_by_language strings present verbatim in flat aliases list.
Set ontology match types to 'closest_parent' or 'none' only — no invented codes.
Included Type 2 Diabetes with human_condition_name 'Type 2 Diabetes' and machine key type2_diabetes per Q7 requirement.
